summary: AI leaders are on the move globally, with Hugging Face's Nathan Lambert touching down in Beijing and Hangzhou to engage with China's AI community—a sign of deepening cross-border technical exchange. Meanwhile, researcher Naomi Saphra sparked curiosity with a cryptic mention of an unconfirmed event at the WHPCD (White House Presidential Council on Diversity), though she pivoted to praising palliative care specialists, leaving the tech world guessing. On GitHub, trending repos reflect a continued focus on open-weight model fine-tuning and agentic tooling, though no single breakout project dominated today's stars. The conversation remains split between geopolitical AI diplomacy and the quieter, essential infrastructure work keeping the ecosystem running.
